title: acer saccharum subsp. grandidentatum
intro: Acer saccharum subsp. grandidentatum, commonly known as the Bigtooth Maple, is a subspecies of maple tree that belongs to the Sapindaceae family. It is a deciduous tree native to the western United States and Mexico, characterized by its large, five-lobed leaves with prominent teeth. The bark and sap of the Bigtooth Maple have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The sap can be prepared as a tea or syrup to aid digestion and reduce symptoms of colds and flu, while the bark can be made into a salve or tincture to soothe sore muscles and joints.
